answer:
In the Smṛti corpus available here (including the Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Manu Smṛti, Parāśara Smṛti, and Viṣṇu Smṛti), *saṃskāra* (or *saṃskāras* in plural) consistently refers to sacraments, purifying rites, or rites of passage. These are Vedic rituals that sanctify and purify the body—especially for the twice-born castes (*dvija*) from conception through major life stages and after ritual lapses—securing spiritual purity in this life and the next.[^1][^2][^3]

Key usages include:
- **Life-cycle and initiatory rites**: Performed "with sacred Vedic rites" for the twice-born "from conception onwards"; marriage is the Vedic *saṃskāra* for women; a Kshatriya receives the "Brahmanical *saṃskāra*" before royal duties.[^4][^5][^6]
- **Purificatory or re-initiatory rites** (*punaḥ saṃskāra* or "new *saṃskāra*"): Remedial rituals after transgressions (e.g., hair-cutting, adopting a false *gotra*, outcaste-like acts, or consuming impurities like excrement/urine/liquor). These restore purity when other penances fail, though some lapses render one irredeemable even by *saṃskāra*.[^7][^8][^9]
- **Funeral and corpse rites**: The "final disposal of the corpse," equated to the fruit of a horse sacrifice if done for the unclaimed.[^10]
- **Prohibitions**: Not performed (nor even observed) for those dying in initiation (*dīkṣā*), with transgression requiring its own *saṃskāra*; fruitless if done under false pretenses.[^11][^12]

No other Smṛti texts in the corpus (e.g., Yājñavalkya, Nārada, Kātyāyana) mention *saṃskāra* in the searched translations. A single non-Smṛti hit equates it to Buddhist "formations" (*saṃskāra*-skandha), unrelated to rites.[^13]

[^1]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-b2f0f2](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b2f0f2).
[^2]: Manu Smṛti 1, [p-c5f30e](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-c5f30e).
[^3]: Manu Smṛti 2, [p-3cd4c5](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-3cd4c5).
[^4]: Viṣṇu Smṛti 22, [p-e7880d](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/22/translation/#p-e7880d).
[^5]: Manu Smṛti 2, [p-c1eab3](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-c1eab3).
[^6]: Manu Smṛti 7, [p-3f62fe](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/07/translation/#p-3f62fe).
[^7]: Parāśara Smṛti 12, [p-1d8d08](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/12/translation/#p-1d8d08).
[^8]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-3b5116](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-3b5116).
[^9]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-f3be7c](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-f3be7c).
[^10]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-e5ca29](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-e5ca29).
[^11]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-b2f0f2](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b2f0f2).
[^12]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti 1, [p-375f43](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-375f43).
